steps.title: Use the timing evidence
steps.content: The common assumption is that photography caused the decline of the portrait miniature. The passage challenges that idea by noting that exhibition submissions for portraits were already decreasing before photography was invented.

steps.title: Draw the supported conclusion
steps.content: Choice B adds unsupported claims about artistic merit. Choice C says miniature portraits lasted longer than assumed, but the passage focuses on the cause of decline. Choice D invents a shift by portrait artists into photography. Since the decline began before photography, other factors may be more responsible. The answer is A.
